message:
fix(content_dedup): force dedup when size > max_inline_size (not only >= min_dedup_size)

`DedupConfig::should_dedup` previously returned true only when
`content_len >= min_dedup_size`, which meant a segment could be classified
as "small enough to inline" (`content_len < min_dedup_size`) while also
being larger than the explicit per-segment inline ceiling
`max_inline_size`. That hit production: when the inline ceiling was
tightened below the dedup threshold (e.g. `min_dedup_size = 128`,
`max_inline_size = 16`), segments in the 17..=127 range got stored inline
in pane records, bloating the storage row and defeating the purpose of
`max_inline_size`.

Change: `should_dedup` now returns true if *either* the dedup threshold
is met *or* the content would exceed `max_inline_size`. The two
thresholds express independent policies (dedup-for-sharing vs
inline-size-cap), and honoring the stricter one keeps both intents.

Tests:

- Unit test `fresh_eyes_config_max_inline_size_forces_store_even_below_min_dedup_size`
  exercises the two-threshold contract directly on `DedupConfig`.
- Unit test `fresh_eyes_content_above_max_inline_size_is_not_inline_even_below_min_dedup_size`
  drives the new path through `DedupEngine::process_segment` and asserts
  the returned `ProcessedSegment` is `!stored_inline` and inserted to
  the store.
- Proptest `prop_should_dedup_threshold` widens its input space to vary
  `max_inline_size in 0..=512` (was fixed at 256) and asserts the new
  disjunction predicate.
- New proptest `fresh_eyes_prop_content_above_max_inline_not_inline`
  fuzzes `max_inline_size in 0..=64` and random `extra` overshoot,
  setting `min_dedup_size = content_len + 1` so the only way the
  implementation can store non-inline is via the max-inline branch.
- Updates neighboring proptest docstrings to reflect the refined
  semantic ("Content below min_dedup_size and within max_inline_size is
  stored inline").

No behavior change when `max_inline_size >= min_dedup_size` (the prior
configuration of most deployments).
